metadata.title: Claude, Sketching.
metadata.description: Skill teardown of the sketch-explainer (Bear's Doodles) pipeline — the MinutePhysics-style progressive-disclosure builder.

skin_warnings: B03: composer beat has an empty spark line — SPARK-LINE LAW wants a short serif cue (e.g. "The change,").

beats.narration_text: Olá — this is Liam, in for Bear. The sketch-explainer skill is a MinutePhysics clone built on one constraint: one sentence, one new element on screen. Nothing disappears — the argument accumulates. This episode is the skill watching itself explain a concept from nothing.

output: Rule: one sentence = one new visual element
Pipeline: script → beats → audio → Manim → assemble

beats.narration_text: The skill lives in skills/make/sketch-explainer/. SKILL.md is the instruction file. Under it: reference/storyboard.md holds the accumulation rules, reference/style.md holds the Bear's Doodles brand — fonts, color, voice ID. Scripts handle each pipeline stage. One folder, one law, one voice.

props.title: One folder, one law.
props.calloutText: reference/storyboard.md is where the ACCUMULATION LAW lives
props.calloutSub: One sentence. One element. No exceptions.
props.sparkLine: The rule is in the folder.

beats.narration_text: The pipeline has five phases. Mode detection first: did you paste a ready script, or raw source text? Mode A skips straight to beats. Mode B writes the Hook, Accumulation, Reveal, and Implication arc. Then beats — the accumulation law applies. Then audio: durations become the master clock. Then Manim renders every beat silent. Then assemble muxes voiceover on. Three gates stop you before each spend.

props.title: Mode first, law second.

phases.label: MODE DETECT
phases.desc:
A: ready script
B: write arc

props.footerNote: Gate 1: script → Gate 2: beats → Gate 3: audio — cheap before expensive.

beats.narration_text: What does the skill actually produce? Run it on entropy — Mode B, raw concept. The skill writes the MinutePhysics arc, applies the accumulation law per sentence, and outputs a beat sheet. The output is the claim.

output: * beat sheet ready — gate 1 awaits approval

beats.narration_text: The beat sheet shows the accumulation law in action. Each beat carries a scene state — the full list of elements visible at that moment — and a new element: the one thing added this sentence. The state grows with every beat. Nothing clears. The constraint is structural.

props.title: beat_sheet.json — sketch-explainer output

beats.narration_text: The accumulation law is the signature mechanism. One sentence equals one new visual element. The state invariant means items never disappear — every prior element stays visible so the viewer watches the argument build. This is what MinutePhysics gets right: you see causality on screen. The constraint is also the pedagogy.

props.heading: The screen never forgets.
props.body: One sentence equals one new visual element. State invariant: nothing disappears. Every prior element stays visible — the viewer watches the argument build, step by step, on screen. Causality is visible because the law makes it structural.
props.quote: One sentence = one new visual element. No exceptions.

beats.narration_text: Three gates, cheap before expensive. Script approved before beats. Beats approved before audio. Audio durations confirmed before any Manim render. A script costs nothing to edit. A Manim render costs compute and time. The gate order is a spend policy. The non-negotiable is plain: never time animation from word-count estimates.

props.heading: Cheap gates guard expensive compute.
props.body: Script before beats. Beats before audio. Audio before render. The sequence is a spend policy — each gate lets you fix something for free before committing to the next cost tier. A gate is not a ceremony; it is where cheap work ends and expensive work begins.
props.quote: Never time animation from word-count estimates.

beats.narration_text: The design tell is what the skill calls optional. SVG icons and hand-drawn doodles are listed as enrichment — transparent overlays, never required. The Manim master ships with or without them. Most beats stay pure Manim. The word 'optional' in the skill is doing real work: it says what the pipeline trusts and what it treats as decoration.

props.heading: Manim ships. Art is optional.
props.body: The non-negotiable: Manim plus voiceover is the complete deliverable. SVG icons, hand-drawn doodles, Higgsfield enhancement — all listed as optional enrichment. They are transparent overlays added after the master ships. Nothing blocks a render. A video with zero icons is still a finished video.
props.quote: The finished video is Manim + voiceover. That is the complete, self-sufficient deliverable.

beats.narration_text: Claude, Sketching. What the skill actually does. One: accumulation law — one sentence, one element, state invariant, nothing disappears. Two: mode detection — paste a ready script and skip to beats; give raw text and get the MinutePhysics arc. Three: three gates, cheap before expensive, each gate signs before the next spend. Four: Manim is the master — SVG and hand doodles are enrichment, never required. The constraint is the pedagogy.

artifactLines: Accumulation law: one sentence, one element, state invariant — nothing disappears.
Mode detection: paste a ready script and skip to beats; give raw text and get the MinutePhysics arc.
Three gates: script, beats, audio — cheap before expensive, human signs each.
Manim is master: SVG and hand doodles are enrichment, never required.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Use the sketch-explainer skill on a concept you can state in one sentence — something with a reveal at the end. Start with: what does the accumulation law force you to cut that you thought you needed? Paste into Claude: dot slash art sketch-explainer, then your concept.

output: Use the sketch-explainer skill on a concept you can
state in one sentence — something with a reveal.
What does the accumulation law force you to cut?
